data.title: Shrub Oak Int'l School reveals renovated kitchen catering to diverse needs incl. autism, completed on Dec. 4, 2025.
data.description: In Mohegan Lake, New York, on December 4, 2025, Shrub Oak International School revealed the completion of its updated kitchen facility. This renovated culinary space was carefully constructed to cater to a range of requirements, including nutritional, sensory, and developmental needs of individuals with autism.

data.title: IMF advises on managing stablecoin risks with strong policies & institutions amidst global regulations.
data.description: One financial institution highlighted the importance of effective macro-policies and strong institutions in addressing the potential risks associated with stablecoins. This response comes amidst diverse global regulations concerning stablecoins.

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield predicts a shift in U.S. CRE market to optimism in 2026 from resilience.
data.description: Cushman & Wakefield projects a shift in the U.S. commercial real estate market from resilience to optimism in 2026. The company anticipates positive growth and increasing confidence among investors. This forecast reflects a promising outlook for the sector, with potential improvements in market conditions.

data.title: NiTEO Products buys Folex from Barrett and Patty Lash. Folex established in 1966.
data.description: NiTEO Products, a prominent company specializing in household and automotive chemical brands, revealed on December 4, 2025, that it has acquired Folexport, Inc. from Barrett and Patty Lash. Folexport, established in 1966 and headquartered in Tualatin, Oregon, is now a part of NiTEO.

data.title: Bitcoin shows potential for bullish December, defies bearish trend. Will it break new highs by 2025 end?
data.description: Bitcoin, which has historically faced a bearish trend during December, is now experiencing a bullish pattern. This shift is a significant deviation from the past ten years. The question arises whether this new trend will lead to higher Bitcoin prices in the coming years. Investors are eagerly waiting to see if BTC will reach new peaks before the conclusion of 2025.

data.title: Bitcoin's next true expansion cycle may not arrive until 2026, as expert predicts high fear, volatility in the market with recent drop below $85,000 and Fear & Greed sentiment gauge at record-low 11.
data.description: According to Alice Liu, the Head of Research at CoinMarketCap, the cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a period of heightened fear and volatility. Liu warns that Bitcoin's next significant growth cycle may not occur until 2026. During an interview at Blockchain week, Liu mentioned that Bitcoin's price fell below $85,000, leading to a record-low Fear & Greed sentiment gauge of 11, indicating "extreme fear" among investors. The anticipated bull run in October did not materialize as liquidity in the market remains low, resulting in exaggerated price fluctuations. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Neurosurgeon Dr. Aaron Cohen-Gadol launches ATLAS Institute of Brain & Spine in Los Angeles, known for minimally invasive techniques.
data.description: Dr. Aaron Cohen-Gadol, a highly esteemed neurosurgeon who received the top award in neurosurgery and established the widely known Neurosurgical Atlas, has revealed the launch of the ATLAS Institute of Brain & Spine in Los Angeles. This new facility will offer Dr. Cohen-Gadol's innovative minimally invasive techniques to patients seeking advanced neurological care.

data.title: Nasdaq edges up, small caps lead with Dollar General and AppLovin breaking out. Jobless claims surprise weekly. Robinhood at key level.
data.description: Today in the stock market, small-cap stocks led the upward trend while the Nasdaq saw a slight increase. This was driven by a surprise drop in weekly jobless claims. Dollar General and AppLovin both experienced significant growth. The Nasdaq in particular showed promising growth, with Robinhood also hitting a key level. This live coverage provided updates on the day's market performance, as reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Blue Gold Limited appoints Rob Simpson as Director of Risk & Digital Operations; Simpson known for work at Bolt.
data.description: In New York City on December 4, 2025, Blue Gold Limited (Nasdaq: BGL) revealed the selection of Rob Simpson as the Director of Risk and Digital Operations. Simpson, renowned for his expertise in financial risk and operational efficiency, notably held the position of Head of Risk and Payments at Bolt. This fintech giant is recognized for its rapid expansion and global influence. Simpson's appointment marks a significant move for Blue Gold, positioning them to capitalize on his proven track record in payments infrastructure and risk management.
